2019-07-01.title: A Global Gay Pride Weekend, in Photos
2019-07-01.abstract: An outpouring of celebration, and a demand for change, 50 years after Stonewall.

2019-07-01.title: In New Talks, U.S. May Settle for a Nuclear Freeze by North Korea
2019-07-01.abstract: The idea taking shape inside the Trump administration would amount to negotiating a nuclear freeze that essentially enshrines the status quo, tacitly accepting the North as a nuclear power.

2019-07-01.title: Hong Kong Protests: Police Crack Down on Demonstrators
2019-07-01.abstract: After hours of protesting in Hong Kong, demonstrators broke into the Legislative Council chambers on Monday. They were later cleared out by riot police who charged the crowd and used tear gas.

2019-07-01.abstract: Hindus and Muslims have long sparred over a few barren acres in the city of Ayodhya. But since Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s commanding election win, Hindus feel a new optimism.

2019-07-01.title: Bombing Kills Dozens and Hurts Schoolchildren as Taliban Talks Resume
2019-07-01.abstract: While negotiators press toward a deal to withdraw U.S. troops, the Taliban set off bombs that wrecked a museum, TV station and a school in Kabul, Afghanistan.

2019-07-02.title: The Government Cut Their Internet. Will Abuses Now Remain Hidden?
2019-07-02.abstract: With ethnic conflict spreading in Rakhine State in Myanmar, a government-led online shutdown could hide human rights abuses and leave vulnerable populations in the dark.

2019-07-02.abstract: Empty tankers, arriving in the Strait of Hormuz, suddenly stop reporting their whereabouts. When they re-emerge on the grid, their tanks appear full.

2019-07-02.title: On the Streets With Hong Kong’s Protesters
2019-07-02.abstract: We saw scenes of violence, as well as hundreds of thousands of peaceful demonstrators. There is still some unity in the face of mainland China’s encroaching influence, but it is less clear how this leaderless protest movement will advance.

2019-07-02.abstract: The destructive turn of the protests on Monday raises questions about a leaderless movement and how long China can stand by.

2019-07-02.abstract: A man’s body fell to earth on a Heathrow Airport flight path. The police say he had probably hidden in a wheel well of an inbound Kenya Airways flight.

2019-07-03.abstract: The question is whether the largely leaderless protest movement can maintain enough unity and public support to push its demands.

2019-07-03.abstract: Iran will “take the next step” on Sunday in enriching uranium beyond levels specified under the 2015 nuclear deal, President Hassan Rouhani said.

2019-07-03.abstract: The bomb ripped through a hangar in Tripoli packed with African migrants hoping to reach Europe. A spokesman for Gen. Khalifa Hifter denied responsibility.

2019-07-04.abstract: Mr. Sigley, whose family raised alarms last week after losing contact with him, declined to say on Thursday why he had been detained.

2019-07-04.title: Photos of Heavy Rains in Japan
2019-07-04.abstract: Some areas have seen as much as three feet of rainfall since the weekend.

2019-07-04.abstract: A meeting between Russia’s president, seen as the spiritual strongman of European nationalists, and the pope, a moral voice against populism, comes amid an ideological divide in the West.

2019-07-04.abstract: Facing the threat of sanctions for not cracking down on militants, Pakistan is again moving against the founder of the terrorist group Lashkar-e-Taiba.

2019-07-05.abstract: Some alliance officials think the move is needed to counter Russia’s new cruise missiles, but it could be “a point of no return with the Russians,” one expert said.

2019-07-06.abstract: Older, whiter and more right-wing than average, Conservatives who will choose their party’s next leader are adamant about one thing: Brexit at any cost.

2019-07-07.abstract: The assassination of a local politician is raising tough questions about the country’s resolve to monitor and control far-right violence.

2019-07-24.abstract: The comments by Junius Ho, widely perceived as threatening toward a pro-democracy legislator, were an escalation of sharp words amid a violent period in Hong Kong.

2019-07-24.title: What Are the Triads, and What Is Their History of Violence?
2019-07-24.abstract: A mob recently beat dozens of protesters in a train station in Hong Kong, renewing fears of organized crime carrying out political violence.

2019-07-25.title: Franky Zapata’s ‘Flyboard’ English Channel Flight Failed. He Vowed to Try Again.
2019-07-25.abstract: The hoverboard creator fell into the water midway through the crossing when he tried to land on a platform to refuel.

2019-07-25.title: A French Town’s Green Policies Aim to Win Over the Working Class
2019-07-25.abstract: In a crumbling industrial area, a mayor turned his town into a vibrant laboratory of pragmatic green policies. After his election to the European Parliament, can they be expanded?

2019-07-25.title: Jordan Creates Artificial Reef From Decommissioned Military Vehicles
2019-07-25.abstract: The Red Sea port city of Aqaba is promoting the environmental and tourism benefits of the new underwater museum.

2019-07-25.title: What Is the Chinese Military Doing in Hong Kong?
2019-07-25.abstract: The People’s Liberation Army has kept a low profile in the city since China resumed control. But the recent protests have some fearing it could emerge from the barracks.

2019-07-25.title: Béji Caïd Essebsi, President Who Guided Tunisia to Democracy, Dies at 92
2019-07-25.abstract: In office at his death, he came out of retirement to lead his country after an Arab Spring uprising ousted a dictator, then won a free election.

2019-07-26.title: Margaret Fulton Brought the World to the Australian Table
2019-07-26.abstract: It’s hard to overstate the cultural impact of the prolific Australian author, who propelled nearly an entire generation toward more open-minded cooking.

2019-07-26.title: New North Korean Missile Comes With Angry Message to South Korea’s President
2019-07-26.abstract: The North said its leader, Kim Jong-un, arranged Thursday’s launch to punish the “double-dealing” of President Moon Jae-in. Analysts detected growing frustration as sanctions bite.

2019-07-26.title: Iran Releases 9 Indian Sailors From Seized Tanker
2019-07-26.abstract: At a time of conflict with the United States and its allies, Iran freed most of the crew to keep friendly relations with India.

2019-07-26.title: British Airways Resumes Cairo Flights After Security Review
2019-07-26.abstract: The airline suspended travel to the Egyptian capital six days ago, after the British government warned of a heightened risk of terrorist attacks.

2019-07-26.title: Hong Kong Protests Spread to Airport as City Fears More Unrest
2019-07-26.abstract: Activists plan to rally Saturday in the same district where a mob attacked protesters last weekend. The police have banned the march, citing the risk of violence.

2019-07-27.title: Clashes and Tear Gas as Hong Kong Police Confront Protesters in Yuen Long
2019-07-27.abstract: Despite a police ban, protesters marched on Saturday in the same area where they were attacked last weekend by people accused of having gang connections.

2019-07-27.title: Is B.D.S. Anti-Semitic? A Closer Look at the Boycott Israel Campaign
2019-07-27.abstract: The Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions Movement aims to press Israel to change its policies toward the Palestinians. It has generated heated debate and much misinformation.

2019-07-27.title: ‘Her Eyes Were Full of Fear.’ Turkey Repatriates Children of ISIS Followers.
2019-07-27.abstract: The country is allowing some of the wives and children of the terror group’s loyalists to return home from Iraq.

2019-07-27.title: Romania’s Police Chief Fired After Officers Took 19 Hours to Respond to Kidnapped Girl’s Calls
2019-07-27.abstract: The case has set off national outrage, with hundreds of people gathering outside the suspect’s house to protest.

2019-07-27.title: 2 American Teenagers Admit Killing Police Officer in Rome, Officials Say
2019-07-27.abstract: The teenagers, 18 and 19, were charged on Saturday over the fatal stabbing of the military police officer after what officials said had been a drug deal gone bad.

2019-07-28.title: Miners Kill Indigenous Leader in Brazil During Invasion of Protected Land
2019-07-28.abstract: Land invasions in indigenous territories are on the rise across Brazil. Indigenous leaders say they regularly come under threat by miners, loggers and farmers.

2019-07-28.title: Under Brazil’s Far-Right Leader, Amazon Protections Slashed and Forests Fall
2019-07-28.abstract: Cutting down trees at the current rate could lead to runaway deforestation, environmentalists say. But President Bolsonaro is sticking to his promise to curb enforcement.

2019-07-28.title: Hong Kong Unrest: ‘We Cannot Give Up’
2019-07-28.abstract: As protests in Hong Kong increasingly escalate into violent clashes, demonstrators, government supporters and others reflect on where the movement is headed.

2019-07-28.title: Hong Kong Protest: Clashes With Police Turn Downtown Into Tear Gas-Filled Battlefield
2019-07-28.abstract: Riot officers also shot rubber bullets and charged protesters near Beijing’s liaison office, sending hundreds of bystanders scrambling for shelter.

2019-07-28.title: Cyprus Rape Case That Roiled Israel Falls Apart, With 12 Suspects Freed
2019-07-28.abstract: A dozen Israeli teenagers accused of raping a British tourist in a Cyprus resort have been freed, and the accuser was arrested on charges of making a false accusation.

2019-07-29.title: Nigerians Flee After Men on Motorbikes Shoot Down Mourners
2019-07-29.abstract: The Nigerian president directed the military to hunt down the perpetrators of the attack, which local officials attributed to the Islamist extremist group Boko Haram.

2019-07-29.title: China Backs Hong Kong Officials, but Leaves Protests for Them to Solve
2019-07-29.abstract: Officials in Beijing defended the Hong Kong government but failed to address demonstrators’ demands for more police accountability and greater democracy.

2019-07-29.title: In an Epic Standoff, Unarmed Algerians Get the Army to Blink
2019-07-29.abstract: They protest twice a week, and the army allows it, wary of a bloody confrontation. So Algeria’s still-bloodless revolution continues.

2019-07-29.title: India’s Wild Tiger Population Rises, Despite Conflict With Humans
2019-07-29.abstract: According to a government estimate, the country now has about 3,000 Bengal tigers, a third more than it had in 2014.

2019-07-29.title: Fears of Navalny Poisoning Are Rooted in Previous Attacks on Kremlin Foes
2019-07-29.abstract: Over a doctor’s objection, the hospital treating the Russian opposition leader Aleksei Navalny sent him back to jail without identifying the cause of his illness.

2019-07-30.title: ‘Please Stop Beating Us’: Where Were Hong Kong’s Police?
2019-07-30.abstract: After a violent mob attack in Hong Kong, protesters accused the police of ignoring their calls for help. We reconstruct what happened that night.

2019-07-30.title: Pakistani Military Plane Crashes, Killing at Least 18
2019-07-30.abstract: The plane crashed into a village near the capital, Islamabad, causing a fire that damaged several homes and killed at least 13 people on the ground.

2019-07-30.title: Burst of Violence Grips Philippine Island, Negros, and Some See Duterte’s Hand
2019-07-30.abstract: Local politicians and educators have been killed in a spasm of violence that has taken 20 lives in the last two weeks on the central Philippine island of Negros.

2019-07-30.title: Doubt Greets China’s Claim That Muslims Have Been Released From Camps
2019-07-30.abstract: The announcement in Beijing was impossible to verify in tightly controlled Xinjiang, and runs counter to accounts of detentions compiled by U.S. officials and activists.

2019-07-30.title: Lawmakers Accuse Trump and Aides of Delaying F-16 Sales to Taiwan
2019-07-30.abstract: China has objected to the proposed sales, and Congress is worried that President Trump and his trade advisers might try to placate Beijing to get a trade deal.

2019-07-31.title: When Trump Tweets, the Editor of ‘China’s Fox News’ Hits Back
2019-07-31.abstract: Hu Xijin, editor of the Global Times, is a combative public voice for Xi Jinping’s government at a time of open rivalry with the United States.

2019-07-31.title: India’s ‘Coffee King’ Found Dead Amid Financial Troubles
2019-07-31.abstract: V.G. Siddhartha beat Starbucks to dominate India’s retail coffee industry, but he also faced growing debt and pressure from the tax authorities.

2019-07-31.title: Jacinda Ardern Is Tested by Construction on Land Held Sacred by Maori
2019-07-31.abstract: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern had promised to do better by the country’s indigenous people than New Zealand’s previous governments.

2019-07-31.title: For Afghan Civilians, Road Between Life and Death Is Narrow — and Mined
2019-07-31.abstract: A bus full of civilians — including university students and families — struck a roadside bomb, leaving 35 people dead in an inferno of fire and blood.

2019-07-31.title: Former Mayor of Tehran Is Sentenced to Death in Wife’s Killing
2019-07-31.abstract: Mohammad Ali Najafi was arrested in May after turning himself in to the police and purportedly confessing to shooting his wife.
